scope: Phoenix product listing for internal catalog and for contract/express flows (user-facing name often 'Get Product List'; no literal GET /product/list in phoenix-core)
entry_points: POST /products/list — ProductsController.list → ProductService.list → ProductRepository.filter (JPQL, paginated ProductListResponse)
POST /products/filter — ProductsController.filter → ProductService.filter → ProductRepository.filter (same query, different permission-derived status sets)

GET /express-contract/list/products — ExpressContractController.productListing → ExpressContractService.getProductsForExpress → ProductRepository.searchForExpressContract (native SQL with validation CTEs)

upstream_dependencies.name: ProductService
upstream_dependencies.usage: Orchestrates admin list/filter: permissions, sort, prompt, passes filters to ProductRepository.filter

upstream_dependencies.name: ProductContractBasicParametersService.getProducts
upstream_dependencies.usage: Resolves logged-in user for searchForContract; builds page request

upstream_dependencies.name: ExpressContractService.getProductsForExpress
upstream_dependencies.usage: Passes customerDetailId, prompt, user id into searchForExpressContract

upstream_dependencies.name: product.products, product.product_details
upstream_dependencies.usage: Core product row, last detail, status ACTIVE, available_for_sale, available_from/to, global_sales_channel, global_segment, contract_type[], payment_guarantee[], term_id / term_group_id, equal_monthly_installments fields, cash/bank guarantee amounts

upstream_dependencies.name: product.product_sales_channels + nomenclature.sales_channels
upstream_dependencies.usage: Channel eligibility; express flow joins portal_tag via customer.account_manager_tags and account_managers for user-scoped portal channels

upstream_dependencies.name: product.product_segments + customer.customer_segments
upstream_dependencies.usage: Segment intersection when not global_segment; allowed_customer_segments CTE in express query

upstream_dependencies.name: product.product_contract_terms
upstream_dependencies.usage: Admin list filters by name/status; express valid_contract_terms CTE: exactly one ACTIVE row per product detail and no CERTAIN_DATE period type

upstream_dependencies.name: terms.terms, terms.term_groups, terms.invoice_payment_terms
upstream_dependencies.usage: Resolved term (direct or via group last detail); valid_terms CTE enforces single payment term value, contract_entry_into_force / supply_activation / start_initial_term_of_contract cardinality and exclusions (EXACT_DAY/MANUAL, EXACT_DATE/MANUAL)

upstream_dependencies.name: product.product_price_components, price_component.price_components, price_component_formula_variables
upstream_dependencies.usage: Express sd_with_null_price_vars excludes products with unresolved formula variables on linked price components

upstream_dependencies.name: product.product_interim_advance_payments, interim_advance_payment.*
upstream_dependencies.usage: Express valid_interim_payments CTE: obligatory payments, term matching or term rows with values, value_type rules, date_of_issue rules

upstream_dependencies.name: product.product_additional_params
upstream_dependencies.usage: Express valid_additional_params excludes details with label set and value null

upstream_dependencies.name: product_contract.contracts, product_contract.contract_details
upstream_dependencies.usage: searchForContract excludes individual products already linked to an active contract in certain statuses for a given customer

upstream_dependencies.name: VwProductContractTerms, VwProductSalesChannels (admin JPQL list only)
upstream_dependencies.usage: Denormalized joins for ProductListResponse columns in filter query

downstream_dependencies.name: phoenix-ui products.service.ts
downstream_dependencies.usage: Calls POST ${api}/products/list for internal energy products grid

downstream_dependencies.name: Product contract & Express contract UIs / flows
downstream_dependencies.usage: GET list/products under /product-contract and /express-contract when creating or changing contracts

downstream_dependencies.name: PaymentPackageController / PaymentPackageService
downstream_dependencies.usage: Imports ProductsListRequest and ProductListResponse patterns for receivable flows

downstream_dependencies.name: Sales Portal (conceptual)
downstream_dependencies.usage: No dedicated GET /sales-portal/.../product/list in phoenix-core; portal-aligned eligibility is implemented via sales channel + account manager tag linkage in searchForExpressContract (and global_sales_channel shortcut)

data_entities: AdvancedPaymentGroup (express product_details CTE references group path for services; product express query focuses on interim paths in snippet)
integration_points: Permission gates: PRODUCT_VIEW_* / INDIVIDUAL_PRODUCT_VIEW_* for /products/list; PRODUCT_CONTRACT_CREATE|EDIT for product-contract listing; EXPRESS_CONTRACT_CREATE for express listing
Account manager ↔ portal tag ↔ sales channel join must stay consistent for express product visibility
Native SQL in searchForContract/searchForExpressContract must stay aligned with JPA entity schema and enum string values in DB
Vw* views used by admin list must match underlying product detail state

what_could_break.item: Internal Phoenix product grid and any automation posting to /products/list or /products/filter
what_could_break.reason: Changes to join paths, Vw* views, filter semantics, or ProductListResponse mapping alter visible rows and columns
SQL changes to availability, segment/channel rules, or individual-product exclusion change which products can be selected
CTE validation (terms, payment guarantee, price variables, interim payments, contract term type) is strict; small data or enum changes can empty the list or admit invalid products

what_could_break.item: Receivable / payment package flows reusing product list models
what_could_break.reason: Request/response shape or permission assumptions tied to product listing

what_could_break.location: Sales channel & segment model + account manager tags
what_could_break.reason: If 'Portals' channel configuration or tag linkage changes, express listing may hide products that should be sellable or show unauthorized channels

technical_details: There is no route exactly named GET /product/list in phoenix-core. The internal catalog uses POST /products/list (see phoenix-ui). Contract-facing 'available products' lists use GET /product-contract/list/products (searchForContract: available_for_sale and date window, global or matched sales channels via account managers, segment rules, excludes individual products already on active contracts) and GET /express-contract/list/products (searchForExpressContract: additional CTEs for single contract_type, single payment_guarantee with amount/currency checks, single non-CERTAIN_DATE product contract term, terms invoice payment term count, contract_entry_into_force/supply_activation/start_initial_term constraints, equal monthly installments conditional, interim/advance payment validation, null price-component variables, null advance price variables, invalid additional params). Literal hardcoded sales channel name 'Portals' does not appear in ProductRepository; portal access is modeled via nomenclature.sales_channels.portal_tag_id and customer.account_manager_tags. Customer Portal-specific product list controller was not found in the searched controllers; Sales Portal product-contract controller exposes CSP document generation, not list/products. PhoenixExpert not invoked (read-only codebase search only).
